author: anonymous student
text:
Review of Marco Martens (Mathematics), MAT. Quality 2.0/5, Difficulty 5.0/5, Grade received: B. This review goes for Fall 2023 MAT-312

Eh... not a great class to start with more proof-based math. This is what got me the most. He never really explained the proofs, just went through them and hoped you understood what he was saying. I was raging at the Homework in the beginning and had to start going to the MLC, but I still got a B-. [Tags: Get ready to read, Lots of homework, Lecture heavy]

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Marco Martens (Mathematics), MAT. Quality 4.0/5, Difficulty 4.0/5, Grade received: B. I really liked Marco Martin's class in MAT131 during the fall semester, and I enjoyed it to a certain extent. He does have an accent and can be hard to understand at first, but it became clearer later. His tests are very hard and he does not provide practice problems. Some questions on the homework don't get covered, so they are hard to complete. [Tags: Lots of homework, Lecture heavy, Test heavy]

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Marco Martens (Mathematics), MAT. Quality 4.0/5, Difficulty 3.0/5, Grade received: A. MAT 131. Go to lectures as they aren't recorded. He curves the midterms like crazy. Success in class mainly depends on TA in recitation but he breaks down material well. [Tags: Lecture heavy]

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Marco Martens (Mathematics), MAT. Quality 2.0/5, Difficulty 4.0/5, Grade received: A. I took his MAT 131 class in my freshman year; he isn't a bad professor, but he isn't a good one either. His tests are very hard, but the good thing about them is that they are heavily curved. His lectures aren't the most interesting, but he does get his point across...eventually. He relies more on examples and practice problems as a way to teach. [Tags: Caring, Lecture heavy, Test heavy]

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Marco Martens (Mathematics), MAT132. Quality 2.0/5, Difficulty 4.0/5, Grade received: A. took him for mat 131, did well bc i spent hours on my own trying to understand the content + all tests are curved. lecture is hit or miss, u'll need the txtbk (don't buy) AND youtube a lot. only 2 exams and the final! tests are wayy easier than the hw, but ur ta makes or breaks ur hw grade. marco's nice + u'll get used to his accent. no livestream. [Tags: Get ready to read, Clear grading criteria, Graded by few things]

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Marco Martens (Mathematics), MAT203. Quality 2.0/5, Difficulty 5.0/5, Grade received: B. Genuinely he is someone who looks to help others. But he rushed through the curriculum, barely explaining how to solve problems.

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Marco Martens (Mathematics), MAT319. Quality 2.0/5, Difficulty 5.0/5, Grade received: C. Professor is very kind and is willing to work with you in office hours, but the material is super difficult and moves fast . The class starts off combined with 320 (the more advanced course) and splits after the 1st midterm. Prof tends to think the material is way easier than it actually is. Show you want to learn and you'll pass even with a C...

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Marco Martens (Mathematics), MAT125. Quality 5.0/5, Difficulty 1.0/5, Grade received: A. Good professor. Show up to lecture and he'll cover all of the material needed to pass. [Tags: Respected, Accessible outside class, Test heavy]

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Marco Martens (Mathematics), MAT125. Quality 1.0/5, Difficulty 4.0/5. In all honesty, he's a nice humble guy. Just not made for lectures. He has a heavy accent so it's hard to understand, as well as being fast-paced. I have to study on my own time in order to catch up with the class and try to get a decent grade... (probably a B-) [Tags: Get ready to read, Lecture heavy, Caring]

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Marco Martens (Mathematics), MAT341. Quality 3.0/5, Difficulty 3.0/5. he is aight [Tags: Accessible outside class, Graded by few things]

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Marco Martens (Mathematics), MAT125. Quality 2.0/5, Difficulty 5.0/5, Grade received: D. Had difficulty understanding him as well as hearing in general. You will definitely need to get outside help if this is your first time taking Calculus [Tags: Skip class? You won't pass., Lecture heavy]

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Marco Martens (Mathematics), MAT125. Quality 2.0/5, Difficulty 4.0/5. Makes a lot of mistakes, difficult to understand, doesn't explain very well [Tags: Skip class? You won't pass., LECTURE HEAVY]

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Marco Martens (Mathematics), MAT125. Quality 2.0/5, Difficulty 3.0/5. After 5 classes I realized it wasn't worth it to go to lectures anymore. He rushes through the material, often makes mistakes, and has a really heavy accent. Also, he isn't good with the mic so you can barely hear him during the lectures.

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Marco Martens (Mathematics), MAT125. Quality 2.0/5, Difficulty 4.0/5. Prof. Martens is a great guy. He's super nice and he's very willing to help but he's just not a good lecturer. He works very fast and his lectures are not very helpful. [Tags: TEST HEAVY, LECTURE HEAVY]

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Marco Martens (Mathematics), MAT125. Quality 2.0/5, Difficulty 4.0/5. He knows his material but not good at explaining it. Often rushes through each problems without explaining the concept behind it. However, he is nice and if you ask him for help hell help you. [Tags: Skip class? You won't pass., LOTS OF HOMEWORK]

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Marco Martens (Mathematics), MAT341. Quality 1.0/5, Difficulty 4.0/5. He's such a great guy. Genuinely cares, genuinely wants to help. But he's a horrible lecturer, and just doesn't know how to run a class. Sloppy handwriting, thick accent, makes frequent mistakes, rushes through material, seems like he really doesn't get the material himself. It didn't help that the book is trash. [Tags: ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS, Caring]

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Marco Martens (Mathematics), MAT341. Quality 4.0/5, Difficulty 3.0/5, Grade received: A. His review sessions are very helpful and he lets you know whats going to be on the midterms. The exam problems are slight variations of the examples in the textbook. Overall hes a good teacher and the class is curved. [Tags: Clear grading criteria, LECTURE HEAVY]

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Marco Martens (Mathematics), MAT341. Quality 2.0/5, Difficulty 3.0/5. The entire class has been about using plug-and-chug methods to solve very basic PDEs. There's 2 problems here: First, plug and chug methods are useless in the real world. Second, we've done no actual studying of PDEs more generally (no formulating, no qualitative analysis, etc), so we come away with a very minimal understanding of anything. Useless [Tags: Clear grading criteria]

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Marco Martens (Mathematics), MAT341. Quality 4.0/5, Difficulty 4.0/5. Lots of work, but put in the effort you'll do fine. He takes time to expain everything in class. If not, you better read the textbook, it moves quick. MAT 341 [Tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK]

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Marco Martens (Mathematics), MAT341. Quality 1.0/5, Difficulty 5.0/5. He rushes through the textbook and doesn't take enough time to adequately explain the material. So far he gives us one example per topic and moves on. He also hasn't updated the course website since 2012. (The schedule still marks one week blank due to Hurricane Sandy. ) [Tags: Get ready to read, LECTURE HEAVY]

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Marco Martens (Mathematics), MAT320. Quality 5.0/5, Difficulty 3.0/5. I thought his lectures were awesome and I was always excited to go to class because they were so interesting. He draws lots of pictures to illustrate concepts, which I really liked. I think he's a great teacher. He was also always extremely helpful during office hours.

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Marco Martens (Mathematics), MAT203. Quality 3.0/5, Difficulty 4.0/5. An overall professor. You get weekly homework so make sure you have the textbook. He is hard to understand and mumbles sometimes so try to sit near the front. He's fast paced but does a lot of examples. The midterms questions won't be nearly as complicated as the homework.

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Marco Martens (Mathematics), MAT211. Quality 2.5/5, Difficulty 4.0/5. Does his best to teach really hard material. Homework and midterm will be very easy. Final will be extremely hard and more about proofs. He is not a bad professor but was unclear about final expectations. For the midterm expect it to be straight forward solving, for the final make sure to memorize definitions rather than how to do problems.

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Marco Martens (Mathematics), MAT. Quality 5.0/5, Difficulty 1.0/5. BEST PROFESSOR EVER!!! He is funny, nice, helpful, and just amazing!!!! I highly recommend to take him for any class available!

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Marco Martens (Mathematics), MAT132. Quality 4.0/5, Difficulty 2.0/5. good teacher, take good notes because he does problems in class. He teaches mostly by examples.

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Marco Martens (Mathematics), MAT132. Quality 3.0/5, Difficulty 4.0/5. VERY nice man but he teaches by examples from the textbook and goes very fast because he knows the material well, but it isn't eay to understand. i find myself just copying everything and not knowing what any of it is about. office hours are a little helpful. tests are SUPER HARD! 3 questions worth 80 points!

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Marco Martens (Mathematics), MAT331. Quality 4.5/5, Difficulty 1.0/5. He is very helpful. Funny accent, but understandable. Very NICE. EASY CLASS. Dont need to know anything about a computer.

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Marco Martens (Mathematics), MAT203. Quality 2.0/5, Difficulty 4.0/5. Let me tell you something about him and the other professor, Fabrizio, who also teaches the same course. The first midterm is gonna be real easy, the second one a tad difficult, but the final is gonna be a nightmare. It covered topics that were not in the hw or in the suggested problems. I got an A in this class. You just have to work real hard.

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Marco Martens (Mathematics), MAT203. Quality 4.5/5, Difficulty 3.0/5. He is really funny and helpful. His accent is a little weird, but u will get used to it soon. Two mid-terms are easy if u master the materials he reviewed before the mid-terms. But the final exam is too hard.

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Marco Martens (Mathematics), MAT203. Quality 4.5/5, Difficulty 2.0/5. I loved him! He has a bit of an accent but is still very understandable. I enjoyed listening to his lectures and he taught the material very well. If you attend lectures and keep up with the work you should be fine. He is definitely my favorite professor so far.

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Marco Martens (Mathematics), MAT211. Quality 4.5/5, Difficulty 2.0/5. one of the best professors

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Marco Martens (Mathematics), MAT125. Quality 2.0/5, Difficulty 3.0/5. Very helpful and friendly. The first thing you'll notice about him is that he can't pronounce words very well, but you get used to it. Exams are quite hard, but the curve should save you in most cases.

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Marco Martens (Mathematics), MAT1. Quality 4.5/5, Difficulty 2.0/5. always at his office hours, always willing to help students. disreguards hard textbooks and teaches his own way. awesome!

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Marco Martens (Mathematics), MAT331. Quality 4.0/5, Difficulty 2.0/5. one of the nicest teachers on campus, always willing to help
